pass1_value.note: Official docs list OAuth2 (Authorization Code and Client Credentials), API Key via X-Api-Key header, and Personal Access Tokens (used as Bearer tokens).

final_value.note: The official DealCloud REST API 'Authentication' documentation describes OAuth 2.0 flows (Authorization Code and Client Credentials), API Key authentication using the X-Api-Key header, and Personal Access Tokens used as Bearer tokens.

pass1_value.note: Docs state API Keys and Personal Access Tokens are provisioned by your DealCloud administrator or by contacting DealCloud support; non-admins must contact support/sales.

final_value.note: The 'Obtain API credentials' documentation indicates credentials (API keys / OAuth client credentials / personal access tokens) are provisioned within DealCloud by an administrator (or via contacting DealCloud/Intapp support), implying non-admin users require admin/support involvement rather than fully self-serve signup.

pass1_value.note: Public docs exist and the REST API is documented, but API credentials must be provisioned by an admin or support/sales contact, preventing immediate self-serve developer access; requires contacting admin or sales.

final_value.note: Although the REST API is publicly documented, obtaining working API credentials requires DealCloud admin provisioning and/or contacting support, so a developer cannot immediately self-serve and build without organizational approval.
